title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Graph Forensics
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G57 is a debug in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

Inspect the Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

content.text: For Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G57 is a debug in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.

stimulus.title: Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

Competency probe: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof. Artifact under review: measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.

options.text: The Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety record says measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety without its stated preconditions, omit the measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety for measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.
The record names the assumption, applies Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-eo2xq7" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bundle Backup Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-eo2xq7
# scenario-key: trace-eo2xq7 probe-7qx2oe boundary-2xq7eo owner-eo2xq7x fault-eo2xq7z
# before-ref: main=a11ceeo candidate=b00b1q7
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11ceeo candidate=c0ffee7
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
